     The Numbers Business Report

BlacKkKlansman (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $49,275,340Details
International Box Office $44,136,086
Worldwide Box Office $93,411,426
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $3,806,592
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,109,108
Total $6,915,700
Further financial details...

Synopsis

It’s the early 1970s, and Ron Stallworth is the first African-American detective to serve in the Colorado Springs Police Department. Determined to make a name for himself, Stallworth bravely sets out on a dangerous mission: infiltrate and expose the Ku Klux Klan. The young detective soon recruits a more seasoned colleague, Flip Zimmerman, into the undercover investigation of a lifetime. Together, they team up to take down the extremist hate group as the organization aims to sanitize its violent rhetoric to appeal to the mainstream.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$10,845,330 (22.0% of total gross)
Legs:4.54 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:52.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$15,000,000 (worldwide box office is 6.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:1,512 opening theaters/1,914 max. theaters, 5.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $61,169,920

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 10th, 2018 (Wide) by Focus Features
January 25th, 2019 (Limited) by Focus Features, released as BlacKkKlansman (2018) (Re-Release)
International Releases: August 9th, 2018 (Wide) (Central America)
August 9th, 2018 (Wide) (Israel)
August 10th, 2018 (Wide) (Finland)
August 16th, 2018 (Wide) (New Zealand)
August 16th, 2018 (Wide) (Middle East Region)
Video Release: October 23rd, 2018 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language throughout, including racial epithets, and for disturbing/violent material and some sexual references.
(Rating bulletin 2537 (Cert #51555), 7/25/2018)
Running Time: 134 minutes
Award nomination: 2019 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Cast, crew, or production detail: Prologue
Geographical setting: Colorado
Plot point: African Americans, Hate Crime, Jewish, Ku Klux Klan, Undercover, White Supremacists
Social setting: Civil Rights Movement
Subgenre: Black Comedy
Time period setting: 1970s, 2010s
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Black Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Monkeypaw Pictures, QC Entertainment, Blumhouse, 40 Acres and a Mule Filmworks, Legendary Pictures, Perfect World Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

It’s the early 1970s, and Ron Stallworth is the first African-American detective to serve in the Colorado Springs Police Department. Determined to make a name for himself, Stallworth bravely sets out on a dangerous mission: infiltrate and expose the Ku Klux Klan. The young detective soon recruits a more seasoned colleague, Flip Zimmerman, into the undercover investigation of a lifetime. Together, they team up to take down the extremist hate group as the organization aims to sanitize its violent rhetoric to appeal to the mainstream.

Opening Weekend:$10,845,330 (22.0% of total gross)

Theater counts:1,512 opening theaters/1,914 max. theaters, 5.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $61,169,920
